name: Scallops with Ginger & Mint
recipeIngredient: 2 medium carrots, peeled and finely grated or shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small, lid-covered pot or saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons butter over low heat. Add the carrots and cover. Cook slowly for about 15 minutes, or until softened.
2. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large skillet over high heat. Pat the scallops dry on a towel. When the oil is nearly smoking, toss the scallops in batches (if necessary) so as not to crowd the pan and cook for 30 seconds without stirring.
3. Stir lightly with a wooden spoon and cook an additional minute. Using a slotted spoon, remove the scallops from the pan and reserve them on a plate. They should be only partially cooked. Discard the oil in the pan.
4. Return the skillet to high heat. Add the shallots, wine, and ginger. Cook until the liquid reduces by half. Meanwhile, the scallops will have released a small amount of liquid. Pour this into the pan, reduce the heat to low, and cook until almost no liquid remains.
5. Return the scallops to the pan, whisk in the remaining butter, and add the mint. Immediately remove from the heat. Taste for salt and pepper.
6. To serve, make a ring of carrots on a platter and mound the sauced scallops in the center.
